algorithms.title: Child BLS Algorithm (1 year to puberty)
algorithms.description: The sequence for a child in cardiac arrest, including single- and multi-rescuer ratios.
steps: Verify scene safety and check responsiveness; shout for help.
Assess breathing and pulse simultaneously (carotid or femoral) for no more than 10 seconds.
If a pulse is present but breathing is absent/inadequate, give breaths at 1 every 2–3 seconds and reassess.
If the heart rate is under 60/min with poor perfusion despite adequate ventilation, begin compressions.
If no pulse, begin CPR: compress the lower half of the sternum with one or two hands, rate 100–120/min, depth at least one-third the chest (~2 in/5 cm), full recoil.
Use 30:2 for a single rescuer and 15:2 for two or more rescuers.
Apply pediatric AED pads/setting as soon as available (adult pads if pediatric are unavailable).
Lone rescuer: witnessed sudden collapse → call and get AED first; unwitnessed → about 2 minutes of CPR first, then call and get AED.
